title: Innovative Mild Egyptian Comte Flatbread
description: A kid-friendly Egyptian dish featuring Comte cheese and a hint of mustard, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This shattering flatbread is also shojin-friendly and features a cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Roll out the dough into a large rectangle and brush with olive oil.
Sprinkle the shredded Comte cheese, mustard, and cure over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling to form a crust.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until it is golden brown and shattering.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
